rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMUNDO LUNA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The trustee was unable to allocate some mortgage payments for the trust's real property between principal and income because the trustee failed to maintain mortgage account statements. 2) The petitioner requests $5,370.75 for her services "as trustee" at the hourly rate of $45. No detailed time records have been provided, but the petitioner's "fee log" shows she spent 119.35 hours during this 2-year account period. 54 hours were spent "getting documents for audit and taxes", and 65.35 hours were spent on "doctor appointments", "prescription drop off and pick up", and "haircuts". 3) The trustee paid $5,180.89 from the trust for expenses of non-trust real property, but no reimbursement to the trust has been made. The trustee requests that the amount be deducted from the trustee's fees to be awarded by the Court on this petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPHINE SIMMONS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Still no proof of service has been filed to show that notice of this (or the original) hearing was given to the executor's prior attorney, William Taylor. Nothing has been filed yet to address the division of the attorney statutory fee, show the itemization of the executor's funds advanced, and to disclose the attorneys' fees requested for extraordinary services. The supplement filed on 11/4/21 does not address these either, so it is unclear if they are now being waived since there are no funds in the estate. If they are to be liens against the real property that is the sole asset of the estate, the Court cannot order them without the amounts being set forth. The supplement also still does not indicate when Harlan Simmons's assignment to Marquez Simmons was filed. None is reflected on the Court's register of actions. Although the only current asset of this estate is the real property located at 750 Mansell Street, SF, the will is a pour-over will to the Simmons Family Trust, and Michael Freedman was appointed as successor trustee of that trust, but he was not given notice. The legal description of the real property must be set forth in the petition and the proposed order. [CRC 7.651(b)(3).] It appears that an amendment to the petition must be filed to address all of the issues. Notice of the petition and amendment must be given to all interested parties, unless waived, after the amendment is E-filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/10/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Courtesy copies and proposed orders should not be stapled together as one document.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 5, 1985 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition (1) To Invalidate First Trust Amendment To 2008 Restatement And Amendment To Williams Family Trust; (2) To Invalidate Improper Transfers To Co-Trustee Due To Undue Influence And Elder Abuse; (3) Invalidate Transfers To And Actions Of Respondent In Capacity As Attorney In Fact; (4) To Invalidate Transfers To Respondent Co Trustee As Self Dealing And Elder Abuse; (5) For Constructive Trust; (6) For Recovery Of Trust Property And Accounting; (7) For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; (8) For Removal Of Respondent
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Vivian Williams's Petition a) The proof of service filed on 10/15/21 does not indicate that a copy of the petition was mailed with the notice, as required by Probate Code Sec. 851(a). b) A copy of any supplement to an 850 petition is also required to be served. [CRC 7.53(b).] c) The supplement, however, was not actually E-filed (available for viewing) until 10/21/21, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. d) Why was Vivian Williams not listed among the beneficiaries? Brian Williams's Petition a) Neither proof of service filed on 8/4/21 or 10/26/21 shows that a copy of the petition (and of the supplement) was served along with the notice [Prob. C. Section 851(a)]. b) The supplement, which also requires notice [CRC 7.53(b).] was not filed until 10/26/21, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing and not E-filed until 10/27/21 (actually available for viewing). Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/13/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

[CRC 7.53(b).] c) The supplement, however, was not actually E-filed (available for viewing) until 10/21/21, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. d) Why was Vivian Williams not listed among the beneficiaries? Brian Williams's Petition a) Neither proof of service filed on 8/4/21 or 10/26/21 shows that a copy of the petition (and of the supplement) was served along with the notice [Prob. C. Section 851(a)]. b) The supplement, which also requires notice [CRC 7.53(b).] was not filed until 10/26/21, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing and not E-filed until 10/27/21 (actually available for viewing). rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA G. VELLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/2021. 1. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]. 2. Notice filed 10/26/2021 must be re-done notice was given prior to the amendment being filed. (See DE-121 page 1 #2). All required parties must receive notice of the amendment not just Artemis Milan. 3. Examiner received unfiled courtesy copies of documents which have been recycled. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/6/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES B. WILLIAMS II
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/2021. 1) Petitioner checked limited IAEA authority on the caption but requested full authority on page 1 #2.c. Please explain. 2) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/29/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLADYS G. GOSZULAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The will is not self-proving. Proof of Subscribing Witness must be filed. Item 3g(1) of the petition was incomplete. There was no Attachment 3g(1)(d) attached to the petition. Item 6 of the petition must be completed, as the decedent was not survived by a spouse, registered domestic partner, or issue. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who are or were the parents and grandparents of the two nieces, who are or were the siblings and parents of the decedent? Are these the only niblings? A proposed personal representative is required to sign and verify her pleadings, including any supplement thereto. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/29/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
